name: Louisiana (United States)
description: A blue field with a heraldic 'pelican in her piety' centred on it: a white pelican in a nest, tearing at her breast to feed three chicks, with three drops of red blood on her chest. Below the nest, a white scroll bears the state motto 'UNION JUSTICE CONFIDENCE' in blue.
history: The pelican-and-motto design has been associated with Louisiana since the early 19th century and was used unofficially as a state flag through the 19th and early 20th centuries. The blue-field flag was officially adopted by the Louisiana State Legislature on 1 July 1912. The design was clarified by Act 92 of 2006 to require three drops of blood on the pelican's chest, after a fourth-grader noticed they had often been omitted in printed versions.

features.role: brown pelican (depicted white with gold accents) tearing at her breast in 'piety' to feed three chicks in a nest, with three drops of red blood falling on her chest
features.symbolism: self-sacrifice — the legend of the pelican feeding her young from her own blood; the brown pelican is Louisiana's state bird

features.role: white ribbon scroll bearing the state motto 'UNION JUSTICE CONFIDENCE' in blue, beneath the pelican
